concept: Mask a polar/charged carboxylate as a neutral, lipophilic ester (e.g. an ethyl ester) to improve oral absorption and permeability; systemic esterases regenerate the active acid.
mechanism: Hepatic and plasma carboxylesterases hydrolyze the ester back to the active carboxylate; the ester form provides the oral bioavailability the polar acid lacks.
precedent_drug: Oseltamivir (ethyl ester of the active carboxylate). Second precedent: enalapril (ester prodrug of enalaprilat).

citation.label: Davies BE. Pharmacokinetics of oseltamivir: an oral antiviral for the treatment and prophylaxis of influenza in diverse populations. J Antimicrob Chemother. 2010;65 Suppl 2:ii5-ii10.
citation.note: Second precedent: MacFadyen RJ et al., enalapril clinical pharmacokinetics, Clin Pharmacokinet. 1993;25(4):274-82, PMID 8261712 | DOI 10.2165/00003088-199325040-00003.
